138	# Add a project-scoped IAM role binding with optimistic concurrency and strong read-back.
139	rbgi_add_project_iam_role() {
140	  zrbgi_sentinel
141	
142	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
143	  local -r z_label="${2:-}"
144	  local -r z_resource="${3:-}"  # resource_base: Base resource URL
145	  local -r z_role="${4:-}"
146	  local -r z_member="${5:-}"
147	  local -r z_parent_infix="${6:-}"
148	
149	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"
150	  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"
151	
152	  local -r z_resource_path="${z_resource#/}"  # strip leading slash if present
153	  local -r z_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/${z_resource_path}"
154	  local -r z_get_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
155	  local -r z_set_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
156	
157	  test -n "${z_resource}" || buc_die "resource required"
158	  test -n "${z_role}"     || buc_die "role required"
159	  test -n "${z_member}"   || buc_die "member required"
160	
161	  buc_log_args "${z_label}: add ${z_member} to ${z_role}"
162	
163	  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
164	  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
165	  # does not apply at project scope.
166	  local -ra z_tolerance=(
167	    "400" "*does not exist*"
168	    "400" "*is not deleted*"
169	  )
170	  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
171	  local z_prop_elapsed=0
172	  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
173	  local z_prop_attempt=0
174	  local z_prop_succeeded=0
175	
176	  while :; do
177	    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
178	
179	    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
180	    buc_log_args "GET_POLICY_URL_DEBUG z_resource:${z_resource} z_get_url:${z_get_url}"
181	    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
182	    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
183	    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
184	    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"
185	
186	    local z_get_code=""
187	    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"
188	
189	    # Check for propagation error on GET
190	    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
191	      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
192	      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
193	        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
194	      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
195	      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
196	      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
197	      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
198	      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
199	      continue
200	    fi
201	
202	    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
203	    rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (get policy)" "${z_get_infix}"
204	
205	    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
206	    local z_etag=""
207	    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing etag"
208	    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty etag"
209	
210	    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"
211	
212	    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON in temp (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
213	    local z_new_policy_json=""
214	    z_new_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" "${z_role}" "${z_member}" "${z_etag}") \
215	      || buc_die "Failed to compose policy JSON"
216	
217	    local z_set_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_set_body.json"
218	    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_new_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}"
219	
220	    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
221	    local z_set_elapsed=0
222	    local z_set_infix=""
223	    local z_set_succeeded=0
224	    while :; do
225	      z_set_infix="${z_parent_infix}-set-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
226	      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"
227	
228	      local z_code=""
229	      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code"
230	
231	      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
232	      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
233	        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
234	        break
235	      fi
236	
237	      case "${z_code}" in
238	        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
239	        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
240	        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
241	        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
242	      esac
243	
244	      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
245	      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
246	      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
247	    done
248	
249	    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
250	    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }
251	
252	    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
253	    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
254	      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
255	    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
256	    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
257	    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
258	    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
259	    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
260	  done
261	
262	  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"
263	
264	  # The setIamPolicy 200 is the authoritative grant confirmation: the policy was
265	  # written atomically with this binding. A post-set read-back verify loop was
266	  # removed here. Against a standing depot, repeated divest/invest cycles leave
267	  # same-email `deleted:serviceAccount:...?uid=` tombstones in the policy (accepted
268	  # — re-levy is quota-limited), and the email→uid read reconciliation can lag the
269	  # getIamPolicy read past any bounded poll, producing a false timeout on a grant
270	  # that already succeeded. The read-back never made the grant effective — that
271	  # propagates on GCP's clock regardless and cannot be accelerated by polling — so
272	  # it was non-load-bearing and is gone.
273	  buc_log_args "${z_label}: granted (setIamPolicy 200)"
274	  return 0
275	}

277	rbgi_add_repo_iam_role() {
278	  zrbgi_sentinel
279	
280	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
281	  local -r z_project_id="${2:-}"
282	  local -r z_account_email="${3:-}"
283	  local -r z_location="${4:-}"
284	  local -r z_repository="${5:-}"
285	  local -r z_role="${6:-}"
286	
287	  test -n "${z_token}"         || buc_die "Token required"
288	  test -n "${z_project_id}"    || buc_die "Project ID required"
289	  test -n "${z_account_email}" || buc_die "Service account email required"
290	  test -n "${z_location}"      || buc_die "Location is required"
291	  test -n "${z_repository}"    || buc_die "Repository is required"
292	  test -n "${z_role}"          || buc_die "Role is required"
293	
294	  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"
295	
296	  local -r z_resource="projects/${z_project_id}/locations/${z_location}/repositories/${z_repository}"
297	  local -r z_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
298	  local -r z_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:setIamPolicy"
299	
300	  buc_log_args 'Adding repo-scoped IAM role' \
301	               " ${z_role} to ${z_account_email} on ${z_location}/${z_repository}"
302	
303	  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s
304	  # plus caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
305	  local -ra z_tolerance=(
306	    "400" "*does not exist*"
307	    "400" "*is not deleted*"
308	    "403" ""
309	  )
310	  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
311	  local z_prop_elapsed=0
312	  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
313	  local z_prop_attempt=0
314	
315	  local z_prop_succeeded=0
316	
317	  while :; do
318	    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
319	    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
320	
321	    buc_log_args "1) GET repo IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
322	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"
323	
324	    local z_get_code
325	    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from repo getIamPolicy"
326	
327	    # Check for propagation error on GET
328	    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
329	      buc_log_args "Repo getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
330	      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
331	        || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
332	      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
333	      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
334	      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
335	      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
336	      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
337	      continue
338	    fi
339	
340	    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
341	    rbuh_require_ok "Get repo IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"
342	
343	    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
344	    local z_etag=""
345	    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing repo etag"
346	    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty repo etag"
347	
348	    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"
349	
350	    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
351	    local z_updated_policy_json=""
352	    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
353	      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "${z_etag}") \
354	      || buc_die "Failed to update policy JSON"
355	
356	    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
357	    local z_repo_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_repo_set_policy_body.json"
358	    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_repo_set_body}" \
359	      || buc_die "Failed to build repo setIamPolicy body"
360	
361	    local z_set_elapsed=0
362	    local z_set_infix=""
363	    local z_set_succeeded=0
364	    while :; do
365	      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
366	      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_repo_set_body}"
367	
368	      local z_set_code=""
369	      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"
370	
371	      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
372	      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
373	        buc_log_args "Repo setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
374	        break
375	      fi
376	
377	      case "${z_set_code}" in
378	        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
379	        409)                 buc_die "Repo IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
380	        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
381	        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set repo IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
382	      esac
383	
384	      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "Repo IAM: timeout setting policy"
385	      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
386	      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
387	    done
388	
389	    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
390	    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }
391	
392	    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
393	    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
394	      || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
395	    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
396	    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
397	    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
398	    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
399	    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
400	  done
401	
402	  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"
403	
404	  buc_log_args 'Successfully added repo-scoped role' "${z_role}"
405	}

# Add a project-scoped IAM role binding with optimistic concurrency and strong read-back.
rbgi_add_project_iam_role() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_label="${2:-}"
  local -r z_resource="${3:-}"  # resource_base: Base resource URL
  local -r z_role="${4:-}"
  local -r z_member="${5:-}"
  local -r z_parent_infix="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"
  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource_path="${z_resource#/}"  # strip leading slash if present
  local -r z_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/${z_resource_path}"
  local -r z_get_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_set_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"

  test -n "${z_resource}" || buc_die "resource required"
  test -n "${z_role}"     || buc_die "role required"
  test -n "${z_member}"   || buc_die "member required"

  buc_log_args "${z_label}: add ${z_member} to ${z_role}"

  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
  # does not apply at project scope.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0
  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))

    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    buc_log_args "GET_POLICY_URL_DEBUG z_resource:${z_resource} z_get_url:${z_get_url}"
    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"

    local z_get_code=""
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (get policy)" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON in temp (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_new_policy_json=""
    z_new_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" "${z_role}" "${z_member}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compose policy JSON"

    local z_set_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_set_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_new_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${z_parent_infix}-set-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_code=""
      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  # The setIamPolicy 200 is the authoritative grant confirmation: the policy was
  # written atomically with this binding. A post-set read-back verify loop was
  # removed here. Against a standing depot, repeated divest/invest cycles leave
  # same-email `deleted:serviceAccount:...?uid=` tombstones in the policy (accepted
  # — re-levy is quota-limited), and the email→uid read reconciliation can lag the
  # getIamPolicy read past any bounded poll, producing a false timeout on a grant
  # that already succeeded. The read-back never made the grant effective — that
  # propagates on GCP's clock regardless and cannot be accelerated by polling — so
  # it was non-load-bearing and is gone.
  buc_log_args "${z_label}: granted (setIamPolicy 200)"
  return 0
}

rbgi_add_repo_iam_role() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_project_id="${2:-}"
  local -r z_account_email="${3:-}"
  local -r z_location="${4:-}"
  local -r z_repository="${5:-}"
  local -r z_role="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}"         || buc_die "Token required"
  test -n "${z_project_id}"    || buc_die "Project ID required"
  test -n "${z_account_email}" || buc_die "Service account email required"
  test -n "${z_location}"      || buc_die "Location is required"
  test -n "${z_repository}"    || buc_die "Repository is required"
  test -n "${z_role}"          || buc_die "Role is required"

  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource="projects/${z_project_id}/locations/${z_location}/repositories/${z_repository}"
  local -r z_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  buc_log_args 'Adding repo-scoped IAM role' \
               " ${z_role} to ${z_account_email} on ${z_location}/${z_repository}"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s
  # plus caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET repo IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_get_code
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from repo getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "Repo getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get repo IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing repo etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty repo etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update policy JSON"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_repo_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_repo_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_repo_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build repo setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_repo_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "Repo setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "Repo IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set repo IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "Repo IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully added repo-scoped role' "${z_role}"
}

rbgi_add_sa_iam_role() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_target_sa_email="${2:-}"
  local -r z_member_email="${3:-}"  # email only; function adds serviceAccount: prefix
  local -r z_role="${4:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"

  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"
  buc_log_args "Granting ${z_role} on SA ${z_target_sa_email} to ${z_member_email}"

  # Caller must have already primed Cloud Build if this is the runtime SA.
  # We do a hard existence check and crash if not accessible.

  buc_log_args 'Verify target SA exists'
  local z_target_encoded
  z_target_encoded=$(rbuh_urlencode_capture "${z_target_sa_email}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to encode SA email"

  local z_verify_code
  rbuh_json "GET" \
    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}" \
                            "${z_token}" "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_IAM_VERIFY}"
  z_verify_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_IAM_VERIFY}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA verify"
  test "${z_verify_code}" = "200" || \
    buc_die "Target service account not accessible: ${z_target_sa_email} (HTTP ${z_verify_code})"

  local -r z_sa_resource="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}"

  # Propagation retry — SA is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET SA IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:getIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
      "${z_get_infix}" "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "SA getIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get SA IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing SA etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty SA etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_member_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update SA IAM policy"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_sa_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build SA setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:setIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
        "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "SA setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "SA IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set SA IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "SA IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully granted SA role' "${z_role}"
}

rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_infix="${1:-}"
  local -r z_role="${2:-}"
  local -r z_member="${3:-}"
  local -r z_etag_opt="${4:-}"

  local -r z_policy_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

  test -n "${z_policy_file}" || return 1
  test -f "${z_policy_file}" || return 1
  test -n "${z_role}"        || return 1
  test -n "${z_member}"      || return 1

  local z_out=""
  z_out=$(
    jq --arg role "${z_role}" --arg member "${z_member}" --arg etag "${z_etag_opt}" '
      # Enforce RBGU standard: version=3 for all IAM policies
      .version = 3 |
      .bindings = (.bindings // []) |
      if ([.bindings[]? | .role] | index($role))
      then .bindings |= map(if .role == $role
                            then .members = ((.members // []) + [$member] | unique)
                            else . end)
      else .bindings += [{role: $role, members: [$member]}]
      end
      # Set etag if provided (optimistic concurrency)
      | (if $etag != "" then .etag = $etag else . end)
    ' "${z_policy_file}"
  ) || return 1

  test -n "${z_out}" || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${z_out}"
}

# Provision service agent (Google-managed service account) for an enabled API.
# Uses serviceusage.googleapis.com generateServiceIdentity to deterministically
# ensure the service agent exists before granting it IAM roles.
# Prints the service agent email to stdout.
rbgi_provision_service_agent() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_api_service="${1}"
  local -r z_project_id="${2}"
  local -r z_token="${3}"

  test -n "${z_api_service}" || buc_die "rbgi_provision_service_agent: API service name required"
  test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "rbgi_provision_service_agent: project ID required"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "rbgi_provision_service_agent: access token required"

  buc_log_args "Provisioning service agent for ${z_api_service} in ${z_project_id}"

  local -r z_infix="provision-sa-${z_api_service}"
  local -r z_url="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/${z_project_id}/services/${z_api_service}.googleapis.com:generateServiceIdentity"

  rbuh_json "POST" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" ""
  rbuh_require_ok "Provision service agent ${z_api_service}" "${z_infix}"

  local z_done
  z_done=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".done") || z_done=""

  local z_final_infix="${z_infix}"
  if test "${z_done}" != "true"; then
    local z_op_name
    z_op_name=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".name") || buc_die "Provision ${z_api_service}: no operation name"
    local -r z_poll_url="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com/v1beta1/${z_op_name}"

    local z_elapsed=0
    while :; do
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))

      z_final_infix="${z_infix}-poll-${z_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_poll_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_final_infix}"

      local z_code
      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_final_infix}") || z_code=""
      test "${z_code}" = "200" || buc_die "Provision ${z_api_service}: poll failed (HTTP ${z_code})"

      z_done=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_final_infix}" ".done") || z_done=""
      test "${z_done}" != "true" || break

      test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
        && buc_die "Provision ${z_api_service}: timeout after ${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s"
      buc_log_args "Provision ${z_api_service}: still running at ${z_elapsed}s..."
    done
  fi

  local z_email
  z_email=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_final_infix}" ".response.email") || z_email=""
  test -n "${z_email}" || buc_die "Provision service agent ${z_api_service}: no email in response"

  buc_log_args "Service agent provisioned: ${z_email}"
  printf '%s' "${z_email}"
}

Before I write the revoke functions, one contract question hinges on `buc_die`'s mechanism — whether a best-effort divest (increment 2) can wrap a faithfully-mirrored revoke in a subshell, or whether the revoke functions must instead return non-zero. Let me check `buc_die`.

content.text: `buc_die` ends in a plain `exit 1` — no parent-signaling trap. So a faithfully-mirrored revoke (buc_die internals) can be wrapped `( … ) || buc_warn` in a subshell at the divest call site for best-effort, which is the increment-2 plan. That settles the increment-1 contract: build the revoke trio as faithful mirrors. Let me get the full function inventory to place them well.

# Compose service account email from name and project ID.
# Args: account_name project_id
# Returns: name@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
rbgi_sa_email_capture() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_name="${1:-}"
  local -r z_project="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_name}"    || return 1
  test -n "${z_project}" || return 1

  echo "${z_name}@${z_project}.${RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
}

7:# Bash Console Guide (BCG) - Pattern Reference
9:## Core Philosophy
21:### Load-Bearing Complexity
23:Every element in a system — every function extraction, every pattern variant, every structural distinction — must carry weight. An element is **load-bearing** when its removal would create a gap between intent and behavior. When similar things differ, ask whether the difference is load-bearing: if yes, document why; if no, homogenize.
25:This is the general decision criterion that tools like Zeroes Theory instantiate. Zeroes Theory asks "how many zeroes did this add to state space?" — load-bearing complexity asks the prior question: "does this element earn its existence at all?"
29:- **Four separate `rbgi_add_*_iam` functions** look like copy-paste, but each wraps a different GCP API with different failure modes. The variation is load-bearing — extracting a "generic IAM helper" would hide which API failed at 3am.
30:- **Three copy-pasted Secret Manager IAM blocks** are NOT load-bearing variation — same API, same failure class, same recovery. Extract a helper.
31:- **Not adding three-part structure to a simple `api_enable` spec definition** — the structure would add ceremony without carrying meaning. Non-load-bearing elements increase cognitive cost without increasing correctness.
33:Non-load-bearing elements are the silent budget overruns of a codebase. Each one is small; their aggregate is what makes systems incomprehensible.
35:## Module Architecture
44:### CLI as Module Gateway
61:# ❌ External script kindling directly
69:# ✅ External script delegates to CLI
91:### Module-CLI Prefix Unity
100:### Regime Module Archetype
121:## Function Patterns
123:### Boilerplate Functions (one per module/CLI)
142:### Regular Functions (output via printouts/temp files)
153:## File Templates
155:### Template 1: Shebang and Copyright
158:#!/bin/bash
159:#
160:# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
161:#
162:# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
163:# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
164:# You may obtain a copy of the License at
165:#
166:#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
167:#
168:# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
169:# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
170:# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
171:# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
172:# limitations under the License.
173:#
174:# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
175:#
182:### Tabtarget Path Indirection
187:#!/bin/bash
201:Why name the launcher in a config variable rather than pass it as a positional dispatch token? **Because the byte-identical exec line is load-bearing.** When the only thing that varies between two tabtargets is their `BURD_*` block, a tabtarget is purely declarative — it states *which launcher and which flags*, and holds no dispatch logic that can drift from the trampoline's expectations. The launcher identity rides the same validated `BURD_*` channel as every other dispatch value rather than a parallel argument slot, and the exec collapses to a true constant the qualifier checks against a single literal.
205:### Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables
217:### Template 2: CLI Entry Point
222:# «shebang»
223:# «copyright»
250:# eof
253:### Differential Furnish (conditional dependency sourcing)
302:### Template 3: Implementation Module
305:# «shebang»
306:# «copyright»
310:# Multiple inclusion detection
314:# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition — available at source time)
315:# «PREFIX»_some_path="fixed-filename.env"
316:# «PREFIX»_some_prefix="prefix_"
318:######################################################################
319:# Internal Functions (z«prefix»_*)
354:# Helper functions follow
356:######################################################################
357:# External Functions («prefix»_*)
385:# ❌ Kindle constant defined outside kindle
390:# ✅ Kindle constants defined in kindle, used elsewhere
400:# ✅ Tinder constant: pure string, available at source time
403:# ✅ Tinder-on-tinder composition: expansion of earlier tinder constant
407:# ✅ Kindle constant: depends on runtime state, requires kindle
410:# ❌ Wrong: runtime variable expansion in tinder position
413:# ❌ Wrong: computation in tinder position
416:# ❌ Wrong: expansion of kindle constant in tinder position
420:## Variable Handling (General Rules)
422:### Expansion Requirements
440:# ❌ Tilde does not expand in quotes
443:# ✅ Use $HOME instead
449:# ✅ Inner expansion quoted separately — matches literally
453:# ❌ Inner expansion unquoted — matches as a glob pattern
460:### Assignment Patterns
463:# Direct assignment
466:# ❌ NEVER declare multiple variables on one line
469:# ✅ ALWAYS one declaration per line with explicit initialization
474:# ❌ NEVER use local -i — silently coerces non-integer values to 0
477:# ✅ Use plain local with explicit validation
481:# ✅ Two-line pattern for captured values (explicit and debuggable)

content.text:
There it is — BCG lines 27-31, the exact example the docket pointed at:

> - **Four separate `rbgi_add_*_iam` functions** look like copy-paste, but each wraps a different GCP API with different failure modes. The variation is load-bearing — extracting a "generic IAM helper" would hide which API failed at 3am.
> - **Three copy-pasted Secret Manager IAM blocks** are NOT load-bearing variation — same API, same failure class, same recovery. Extract a helper.

This directly confirms the design: three separate revoke functions (CRM / ArtifactRegistry / IAM — genuinely different APIs, tolerances, and the SA-verify preflight) are load-bearing; the shared jq helper (pure JSON transform, no API) is the legitimate extraction. Now let me read the BCG sections I actually need to write conformant bash — constants, variable handling, and shellcheck.

content.content:
121	## Function Patterns
122	
123	### Boilerplate Functions (one per module/CLI)
124	
125	| Function             | Location       | First Line                                         | Can Source?       | Can use buc_step? | Purpose                                          |
126	|----------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
127	| `z«prefix»_kindle`   | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | Yes (credentials) | No (use buc_log_*)| Define all kindle constants, set module state    |
128	| `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Implementation | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | No                | No                | Guard all other functions                        |
129	| `z«prefix»_enforce`  | Implementation | `z«prefix»_sentinel`                               | No                | No                | Validate sourced config (regime archetype only)  |
130	| `z«prefix»_furnish`  | CLI only       | `buc_doc_env...`                                   | Yes (all deps)    | No                | Document env vars, source all deps, kindle       |
131	| Module header        | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | No                | N/A               | Prevent multiple inclusion                       |
132	| CLI header           | CLI            | `set -euo pipefail`                                | Yes (buc_command)  | N/A               | Source buc_command.sh only                       |
133	
134	**Kindle constant**: Any variable — internal (`Z«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) or public (`«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) — defined exclusively within the kindle function with `readonly`. No other function — including enroll, setup, or helper functions — may assign to kindle constants. This ensures module state is fully determined at kindle time and visible in one place.
135	
136	**Mutable kindle state**: A variable initialized in kindle but intentionally mutated after kindle returns (counters, accumulators, registry rolls, builder state). Uses **lowercase** `z_«prefix»_name` to visually distinguish from `readonly` kindle constants. Never apply `readonly` to mutable kindle state.
137	
138	**Tinder constant**: A public variable (`«PREFIX»_lower_name`) defined at module top level, immediately after the `Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1` guard. Tinder constants must resolve to pure string literals at source time — **no runtime dependency, no `$(…)` computation, no expansion of kindle constants or runtime variables**. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is permitted: a tinder value may interpolate `${«PREFIX»_lower_name}` for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves the no-runtime-dependency invariant. They are available immediately after sourcing — no kindle required. Use `SCREAMING` case for the prefix (module identity) and `lower_snake` case for the name to visually distinguish from kindle constants. This enables sourcing chains where a tinder constant from module A provides the path for sourcing module B's config in the furnish function. The name reflects their role in the kindle metaphor: tinder is the fixed material prepared before kindling — inert, unchanging, and enabling the fire to start.
139	
140	**KINDLED must be last**: `readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1` must be the final statement in kindle. Since sentinel checks this variable, setting it last guarantees all kindle constants, roll arrays, and enroll calls are complete before the module is considered operational. Any function calling sentinel before kindle finishes will correctly fail. The `readonly` makes re-kindling a loud error.
141	
142	### Regular Functions (output via printouts/temp files)
143	
144	This table defines scope: «prefix»_* is public, z«prefix»_* is internal.
145	
146	| Type            | Pattern              | First Line           | Parameters | buc_step | Documentation        |
147	|-----------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------|----------|----------------------|
148	| Public          | `«prefix»_«command»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Required (buc_doc_*) |
149	| Internal helper | `z«prefix»_«name»`   | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Recommended (buc_doc_*) |
150	
151	---
152	
153	## File Templates
154	
155	### Template 1: Shebang and Copyright

## Function Patterns

### Boilerplate Functions (one per module/CLI)

| Function             | Location       | First Line                                         | Can Source?       | Can use buc_step? | Purpose                                          |
|----------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `z«prefix»_kindle`   | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | Yes (credentials) | No (use buc_log_*)| Define all kindle constants, set module state    |
| `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Implementation | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | No                | No                | Guard all other functions                        |
| `z«prefix»_enforce`  | Implementation | `z«prefix»_sentinel`                               | No                | No                | Validate sourced config (regime archetype only)  |
| `z«prefix»_furnish`  | CLI only       | `buc_doc_env...`                                   | Yes (all deps)    | No                | Document env vars, source all deps, kindle       |
| Module header        | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | No                | N/A               | Prevent multiple inclusion                       |
| CLI header           | CLI            | `set -euo pipefail`                                | Yes (buc_command)  | N/A               | Source buc_command.sh only                       |

**Kindle constant**: Any variable — internal (`Z«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) or public (`«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) — defined exclusively within the kindle function with `readonly`. No other function — including enroll, setup, or helper functions — may assign to kindle constants. This ensures module state is fully determined at kindle time and visible in one place.

**Mutable kindle state**: A variable initialized in kindle but intentionally mutated after kindle returns (counters, accumulators, registry rolls, builder state). Uses **lowercase** `z_«prefix»_name` to visually distinguish from `readonly` kindle constants. Never apply `readonly` to mutable kindle state.

**Tinder constant**: A public variable (`«PREFIX»_lower_name`) defined at module top level, immediately after the `Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1` guard. Tinder constants must resolve to pure string literals at source time — **no runtime dependency, no `$(…)` computation, no expansion of kindle constants or runtime variables**. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is permitted: a tinder value may interpolate `${«PREFIX»_lower_name}` for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves the no-runtime-dependency invariant. They are available immediately after sourcing — no kindle required. Use `SCREAMING` case for the prefix (module identity) and `lower_snake` case for the name to visually distinguish from kindle constants. This enables sourcing chains where a tinder constant from module A provides the path for sourcing module B's config in the furnish function. The name reflects their role in the kindle metaphor: tinder is the fixed material prepared before kindling — inert, unchanging, and enabling the fire to start.

**KINDLED must be last**: `readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1` must be the final statement in kindle. Since sentinel checks this variable, setting it last guarantees all kindle constants, roll arrays, and enroll calls are complete before the module is considered operational. Any function calling sentinel before kindle finishes will correctly fail. The `readonly` makes re-kindling a loud error.

### Regular Functions (output via printouts/temp files)

This table defines scope: «prefix»_* is public, z«prefix»_* is internal.

| Type            | Pattern              | First Line           | Parameters | buc_step | Documentation        |
|-----------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------|----------|----------------------|
| Public          | `«prefix»_«command»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Required (buc_doc_*) |
| Internal helper | `z«prefix»_«name»`   | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Recommended (buc_doc_*) |

---

## File Templates

### Template 1: Shebang and Copyright

content.content:
420	## Variable Handling (General Rules)
421	
422	### Expansion Requirements
423	
424	**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
425	```bash
426	"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
427	"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
428	"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
429	```
430	
431	**Never use:**
432	```bash
433	$var                    # Unquoted - word splitting risk
434	"$var"                  # Unbraced - boundary ambiguity
435	${var}                  # Unquoted - glob expansion risk
436	```
437	
438	**No tilde in quoted strings** — tilde expansion is suppressed inside quotes:
439	```bash
440	# ❌ Tilde does not expand in quotes
441	"~/Downloads/file.txt"          # Literal ~ character, not home directory
442	
443	# ✅ Use $HOME instead
444	"${HOME}/Downloads/file.txt"    # Expands correctly
445	```
446	
447	**Quote inside parameter expansion operators:**
448	```bash
449	# ✅ Inner expansion quoted separately — matches literally
450	"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"       # Strip suffix
451	"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"       # Strip prefix
452	
453	# ❌ Inner expansion unquoted — matches as a glob pattern
454	"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"         # z_suffix with * or ? chars matches as glob!
455	"${z_path#${z_prefix}}"         # Same risk
456	```
457	
458	When using `%`, `%%`, `#`, `##` operators with a variable as the pattern, the inner expansion must be quoted separately. Without inner quotes, the pattern is glob-expanded — a suffix containing `*` or `?` silently matches more than intended.
459	
460	### Assignment Patterns
461	
462	```bash
463	# Direct assignment
464	local z_message="Processing item"
465	
466	# ❌ NEVER declare multiple variables on one line
467	local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!
468	
469	# ✅ ALWAYS one declaration per line with explicit initialization
470	local z_name=""
471	local z_value="default"
472	local z_result=0
473	
474	# ❌ NEVER use local -i — silently coerces non-integer values to 0
475	local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0 — violates no-silent-failures!
476	
477	# ✅ Use plain local with explicit validation
478	local z_count="${z_input}"
479	test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "z_count must be non-negative integer, got: ${z_count}"
480	
481	# ✅ Two-line pattern for captured values (explicit and debuggable)
482	local z_token
483	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
484	
485	# ❌ NEVER combine declaration with capture
486	local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!
487	
488	# From file using builtin $(<file) - NOT command substitution, a bash builtin
489	local z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
490	test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"
491	
492	# Never split declaration from $(<file) assignment
493	# ❌ local z_content
494	# ❌ z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")  # Unnecessary verbosity
495	
496	# Use Capture functions for secrets that must never touch disk
497	local z_token
498	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
499	
500	# Temp files INSTEAD of command substitution holds intermediate results
501	echo "${z_result}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE}"
502	complex_command > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
503	process < "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
504	local z_result=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}")
505	test -n "${z_result}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}"
506	```
507	
508	### Prefer Bash Builtins Over External Tools
509	
510	```bash
511	# ✅ String manipulation with parameter expansion
512	"${z_path##*/}"                    # Instead of: basename
513	"${z_path%/*}"                     # Instead of: dirname
514	"${z_string//old/new}"             # Instead of: sed 's/old/new/g'
515	"${z_string#prefix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/^prefix//'
516	"${z_string%suffix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/suffix$//'
517	
518	# ✅ Reading lines with 'read' — guard handles missing trailing newline
519	while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
520	  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo line"
521	done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"
522	
523	# ✅ Arithmetic with $((...))
524	z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))    # Instead of: expr or bc
525	```
526	
527	### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution
528	
529	All external command output capture uses temp files. The three prohibited `$()` patterns are: `local z_var=$(cmd)` (local swallows exit status), pipelines inside `$()` (hidden intermediate failures), and unguarded `$()` (silent failure). The only `$()` permitted on functions is for `_capture` (secrets that must not touch disk) and `_recite` (read-only roll access) — both with explicit `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`. Bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`) is also permitted in `$()` as no file-based alternative exists.

## Variable Handling (General Rules)

### Expansion Requirements

**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
```bash
"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
```

**Never use:**
```bash
$var                    # Unquoted - word splitting risk
"$var"                  # Unbraced - boundary ambiguity
${var}                  # Unquoted - glob expansion risk
```

**No tilde in quoted strings** — tilde expansion is suppressed inside quotes:
```bash
# ❌ Tilde does not expand in quotes
"~/Downloads/file.txt"          # Literal ~ character, not home directory

# ✅ Use $HOME instead
"${HOME}/Downloads/file.txt"    # Expands correctly
```

**Quote inside parameter expansion operators:**
```bash
# ✅ Inner expansion quoted separately — matches literally
"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"       # Strip suffix
"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"       # Strip prefix

# ❌ Inner expansion unquoted — matches as a glob pattern
"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"         # z_suffix with * or ? chars matches as glob!
"${z_path#${z_prefix}}"         # Same risk
```

When using `%`, `%%`, `#`, `##` operators with a variable as the pattern, the inner expansion must be quoted separately. Without inner quotes, the pattern is glob-expanded — a suffix containing `*` or `?` silently matches more than intended.

### Assignment Patterns

```bash
# Direct assignment
local z_message="Processing item"

# ❌ NEVER declare multiple variables on one line
local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!

# ✅ ALWAYS one declaration per line with explicit initialization
local z_name=""
local z_value="default"
local z_result=0

# ❌ NEVER use local -i — silently coerces non-integer values to 0
local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0 — violates no-silent-failures!

# ✅ Use plain local with explicit validation
local z_count="${z_input}"
test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "z_count must be non-negative integer, got: ${z_count}"

# ✅ Two-line pattern for captured values (explicit and debuggable)
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"

# ❌ NEVER combine declaration with capture
local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!

# From file using builtin $(<file) - NOT command substitution, a bash builtin
local z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"

# Never split declaration from $(<file) assignment
# ❌ local z_content
# ❌ z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")  # Unnecessary verbosity

# Use Capture functions for secrets that must never touch disk
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"

# Temp files INSTEAD of command substitution holds intermediate results
echo "${z_result}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE}"
complex_command > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
process < "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
local z_result=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}")
test -n "${z_result}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}"
```

### Prefer Bash Builtins Over External Tools

```bash
# ✅ String manipulation with parameter expansion
"${z_path##*/}"                    # Instead of: basename
"${z_path%/*}"                     # Instead of: dirname
"${z_string//old/new}"             # Instead of: sed 's/old/new/g'
"${z_string#prefix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/^prefix//'
"${z_string%suffix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/suffix$//'

# ✅ Reading lines with 'read' — guard handles missing trailing newline
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo line"
done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"

# ✅ Arithmetic with $((...))
z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))    # Instead of: expr or bc
```

### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution

All external command output capture uses temp files. The three prohibited `$()` patterns are: `local z_var=$(cmd)` (local swallows exit status), pipelines inside `$()` (hidden intermediate failures), and unguarded `$()` (silent failure). The only `$()` permitted on functions is for `_capture` (secrets that must not touch disk) and `_recite` (read-only roll access) — both with explicit `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`. Bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`) is also permitted in `$()` as no file-based alternative exists.

content.content:
178:**Cross-module references need no per-file directive.** Files that reference cross-module `Z*_/RB*_` readonly state trip SC2153 (possible misspelling) and SC2154 (referenced but not assigned). Both are suppressed globally in `busc_shellcheckrc`, applied on the canonical lint path (the project's shellcheck tabtarget always passes `--rcfile`). No inline directive is carried — see **Shellcheck Integration** and **Prohibited Constructs § Inline Shellcheck Directives**.
1147:- **Isolation subshells** — environment containment with exit-status propagation (below)
1158:# Variant 1: Error propagation — die on any internal failure
1240:Bare `> file` without a command is valid bash but reads like an accident at 3am. The `: >` form explicitly signals "I am creating or truncating this file." Shellcheck enforces this via SC2188.
1244:### ❌ Inline Shellcheck Directives
1247:# ❌ Never suppress shellcheck inline
1248:# shellcheck disable=SC2086
1252:# shellcheck source=/dev/null
1256:All BCG-structural shellcheck suppressions live in `busc_shellcheckrc`. No inline `# shellcheck` directives of any kind. If a shellcheck code fires and it's BCG-structural, add it to `busc_shellcheckrc` with rationale. If it's a genuine finding, fix the code.
1884:## Shellcheck Integration
1886:BCG uses [shellcheck](https://www.shellcheck.net) for static analysis of bash source with a curated suppress list that eliminates false positives caused by BCG's module architecture.
1892:| `busc_shellcheckrc` | `Tools/buk/` | BCG-structural suppressions with rationale |
1893:| `buq_shellcheck` | `Tools/buk/buq_qualify.sh` | Reusable BUK shellcheck engine; hard-requires shellcheck (`buc_die` when absent) |
1895:A consuming kit invokes `buq_shellcheck` from its own qualification gates — typically a standalone lint entry plus the heavier release and marshal gates, never the routine development path. That wiring lives in the consumer; BCG stays unaware of any specific kit's tabtargets.
1916:1. **No inline directives.** All structural suppressions live in `busc_shellcheckrc`. See **Prohibited Constructs § Inline Shellcheck Directives**.
1917:2. **Genuine findings must be fixed.** If shellcheck flags code that isn't BCG-structural, the code has a real issue.
1918:3. **New structural codes** discovered during adoption should be added to `busc_shellcheckrc` with rationale — never suppressed inline.

- **Load-Bearing Complexity** (the literal `rbgi_add_*_iam` example): three separate revoke functions are load-bearing (CRM / ArtifactRegistry / IAM are different APIs with different tolerances, and SA-revoke needs the resource-exists preflight); the shared jq helper is the legitimate "same transform, no API" extraction — the Secret-Manager case where you *should* extract.
- **Variable Handling**: braced/quoted expansion, one `local` per line with explicit init, two-line capture pattern, no `local -i`, never combine declaration with capture.
