content.text: Now the revoke trio, inserted after the grant cluster (after `rbgi_grant_secret_iam`, before `rbgi_sa_email_capture`):

input.new_string:
buc_log_args "Successfully granted secret role ${z_role}"
}

######################################################################
# Revoke (member removal) — inverse of the add_*_iam_role trio.
#
# Each is the one-for-one mirror of its grant counterpart (BCG load-bearing:
# CRM / ArtifactRegistry / IAM are distinct APIs with distinct failure modes,
# so they stay three functions, not a generic helper). Same GET -> modify ->
# setIamPolicy envelope and propagation tolerance; only the jq transform
# (remove instead of add) and the labels differ. Removing an absent member is a
# no-op (jq subtraction), so revoke is idempotent. buc_die on hard failure —
# the teardown call site decides best-effort by wrapping in a subshell.

# Revoke a project-scoped IAM member binding — inverse of rbgi_add_project_iam_role.
rbgi_revoke_project_member() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_label="${2:-}"
  local -r z_resource="${3:-}"  # resource_base: Base resource URL
  local -r z_role="${4:-}"
  local -r z_member="${5:-}"
  local -r z_parent_infix="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"
  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource_path="${z_resource#/}"  # strip leading slash if present
  local -r z_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/${z_resource_path}"
  local -r z_get_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_set_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"

  test -n "${z_resource}" || buc_die "resource required"
  test -n "${z_role}"     || buc_die "role required"
  test -n "${z_member}"   || buc_die "member required"

  buc_log_args "${z_label}: revoke ${z_member} from ${z_role}"

  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
  # does not apply at project scope.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0
  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))

    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"

    local z_get_code=""
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (get policy)" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON in temp (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_new_policy_json=""
    z_new_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" "${z_role}" "${z_member}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compose policy JSON"

    local z_set_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_set_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_new_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${z_parent_infix}-set-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_code=""
      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args "${z_label}: revoked (setIamPolicy 200)"
  return 0
}

# Revoke a repo-scoped IAM member binding — inverse of rbgi_add_repo_iam_role.
rbgi_revoke_repo_member() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_project_id="${2:-}"
  local -r z_account_email="${3:-}"
  local -r z_location="${4:-}"
  local -r z_repository="${5:-}"
  local -r z_role="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}"         || buc_die "Token required"
  test -n "${z_project_id}"    || buc_die "Project ID required"
  test -n "${z_account_email}" || buc_die "Service account email required"
  test -n "${z_location}"      || buc_die "Location is required"
  test -n "${z_repository}"    || buc_die "Repository is required"
  test -n "${z_role}"          || buc_die "Role is required"

  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource="projects/${z_project_id}/locations/${z_location}/repositories/${z_repository}"
  local -r z_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  buc_log_args 'Revoking repo-scoped IAM role' \
               " ${z_role} from ${z_account_email} on ${z_location}/${z_repository}"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s
  # plus caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_REVOKE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET repo IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_get_code
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from repo getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "Repo getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get repo IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing repo etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty repo etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update policy JSON"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_repo_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_repo_revoke_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_repo_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build repo setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_REVOKE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_repo_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "Repo setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "Repo IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set repo IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "Repo IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully revoked repo-scoped role' "${z_role}"
}

# Revoke an SA-scoped IAM member binding — inverse of rbgi_add_sa_iam_role.
rbgi_revoke_sa_member() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_target_sa_email="${2:-}"
  local -r z_member_email="${3:-}"  # email only; function adds serviceAccount: prefix
  local -r z_role="${4:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"

  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"
  buc_log_args "Revoking ${z_role} on SA ${z_target_sa_email} from ${z_member_email}"

  # Hard existence check on the target (resource) SA — mirror of the grant path.
  # If the resource SA is gone its policy is gone too; a best-effort teardown
  # caller absorbs that buc_die via subshell.
  buc_log_args 'Verify target SA exists'
  local z_target_encoded
  z_target_encoded=$(rbuh_urlencode_capture "${z_target_sa_email}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to encode SA email"

  local z_verify_code
  rbuh_json "GET" \
    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}" \
                            "${z_token}" "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_VERIFY}"
  z_verify_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_VERIFY}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA verify"
  test "${z_verify_code}" = "200" || \
    buc_die "Target service account not accessible: ${z_target_sa_email} (HTTP ${z_verify_code})"

  local -r z_sa_resource="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}"

  # Propagation retry — SA is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET SA IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:getIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
      "${z_get_infix}" "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "SA getIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get SA IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing SA etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty SA etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_member_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update SA IAM policy"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_sa_revoke_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build SA setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:setIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
        "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "SA setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "SA IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set SA IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "SA IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully revoked SA role' "${z_role}"
}

######################################################################
# Revoke (member removal) — inverse of the add_*_iam_role trio.
#
# Each is the one-for-one mirror of its grant counterpart (BCG load-bearing:
# CRM / ArtifactRegistry / IAM are distinct APIs with distinct failure modes,
# so they stay three functions, not a generic helper). Same GET -> modify ->
# setIamPolicy envelope and propagation tolerance; only the jq transform
# (remove instead of add) and the labels differ. Removing an absent member is a
# no-op (jq subtraction), so revoke is idempotent. buc_die on hard failure —
# the teardown call site decides best-effort by wrapping in a subshell.

# Revoke a project-scoped IAM member binding — inverse of rbgi_add_project_iam_role.
rbgi_revoke_project_member() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_label="${2:-}"
  local -r z_resource="${3:-}"  # resource_base: Base resource URL
  local -r z_role="${4:-}"
  local -r z_member="${5:-}"
  local -r z_parent_infix="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"
  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource_path="${z_resource#/}"  # strip leading slash if present
  local -r z_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/${z_resource_path}"
  local -r z_get_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_set_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"

  test -n "${z_resource}" || buc_die "resource required"
  test -n "${z_role}"     || buc_die "role required"
  test -n "${z_member}"   || buc_die "member required"

  buc_log_args "${z_label}: revoke ${z_member} from ${z_role}"

  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
  # does not apply at project scope.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0
  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))

    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"

    local z_get_code=""
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (get policy)" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON in temp (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_new_policy_json=""
    z_new_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" "${z_role}" "${z_member}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compose policy JSON"

    local z_set_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_set_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_new_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${z_parent_infix}-set-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_code=""
      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args "${z_label}: revoked (setIamPolicy 200)"
  return 0
}

# Revoke a repo-scoped IAM member binding — inverse of rbgi_add_repo_iam_role.
rbgi_revoke_repo_member() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_project_id="${2:-}"
  local -r z_account_email="${3:-}"
  local -r z_location="${4:-}"
  local -r z_repository="${5:-}"
  local -r z_role="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}"         || buc_die "Token required"
  test -n "${z_project_id}"    || buc_die "Project ID required"
  test -n "${z_account_email}" || buc_die "Service account email required"
  test -n "${z_location}"      || buc_die "Location is required"
  test -n "${z_repository}"    || buc_die "Repository is required"
  test -n "${z_role}"          || buc_die "Role is required"

  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"

  local -r z_resource="projects/${z_project_id}/locations/${z_location}/repositories/${z_repository}"
  local -r z_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  buc_log_args 'Revoking repo-scoped IAM role' \
               " ${z_role} from ${z_account_email} on ${z_location}/${z_repository}"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s
  # plus caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_REVOKE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET repo IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_get_code
    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from repo getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "Repo getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get repo IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing repo etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty repo etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update policy JSON"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_repo_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_repo_revoke_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_repo_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build repo setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_REVOKE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_repo_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "Repo setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "Repo IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set repo IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "Repo IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "Repo IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully revoked repo-scoped role' "${z_role}"
}

                            "${z_token}" "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_VERIFY}"
  z_verify_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_VERIFY}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA verify"
  test "${z_verify_code}" = "200" || \

  local -r z_sa_resource="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}"

  # Propagation retry — SA is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}

    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET SA IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:getIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
      "${z_get_infix}" "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "SA getIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
    rbuh_require_ok "Get SA IAM policy" "${z_get_infix}"

    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
    local z_etag=""
    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing SA etag"
    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty SA etag"

    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"

    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON (member removed; version=3; keep etag)'
    local z_updated_policy_json=""
    z_updated_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" \
      "${z_role}" "serviceAccount:${z_member_email}" "${z_etag}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to update SA IAM policy"

    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
    local z_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_sa_revoke_set_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_updated_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to build SA setIamPolicy body"

    local z_set_elapsed=0
    local z_set_infix=""
    local z_set_succeeded=0
    while :; do
      z_set_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_REVOKE_SET}-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:setIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
        "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"

      local z_set_code=""
      z_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from setIamPolicy"

      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
        buc_log_args "SA setIamPolicy returned ${z_set_code} (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "SA IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "Set SA IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "SA IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

  buc_log_args 'Successfully revoked SA role' "${z_role}"
}

# Compose service account email from name and project ID.

# Remove a member from a role binding with version=3 enforcement — inverse of
# rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture. This is the one legitimate shared helper
# (BCG load-bearing: pure JSON transform, no API, same failure class for every
# scope). jq subtraction drops all occurrences of the member, so an absent
# member is a no-op (idempotent). A binding left with no members is pruned —
# setIamPolicy rejects an empty members list; only the named role can empty out,
# since other bindings are untouched and arrive non-empty from getIamPolicy.
#
# Args: infix role member [etag_optional]
# Returns: JSON policy string with member removed and version=3
rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture() {
  zrbgi_sentinel

  local -r z_infix="${1:-}"
  local -r z_role="${2:-}"
  local -r z_member="${3:-}"
  local -r z_etag_opt="${4:-}"

  local -r z_policy_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

  test -n "${z_policy_file}" || return 1
  test -f "${z_policy_file}" || return 1
  test -n "${z_role}"        || return 1
  test -n "${z_member}"      || return 1

  local z_out=""
  z_out=$(
    jq --arg role "${z_role}" --arg member "${z_member}" --arg etag "${z_etag_opt}" '
      # Enforce RBGU standard: version=3 for all IAM policies
      .version = 3 |
      .bindings = (.bindings // []) |
      # Drop the member from the named role, then prune any binding left empty
      .bindings |= ( map(if .role == $role
                         then .members = ((.members // []) - [$member])
                         else . end)
                     | map(select((.members // []) | length > 0)) )
      # Set etag if provided (optimistic concurrency)
      | (if $etag != "" then .etag = $etag else . end)
    ' "${z_policy_file}"
  ) || return 1

  test -n "${z_out}" || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${z_out}"
}

# Provision service agent (Google-managed service account) for an enabled API.

message.content: That is obviously not BCG compliant.

content.text:
You're right. Let me say plainly what I now see, because I missed it twice and I want to name it exactly rather than wave at it.

What I did: I mirrored the three `rbgi_add_*_iam_role` bodies *wholesale* into the revokes — copying the entire propagation-visibility retry envelope (the `400 "*does not exist*"` / `"*is not deleted*"` tolerances, the exponential backoff outer loop, the `403` resource-cache tolerance). ~130 lines of it, three times.

But that machinery is load-bearing **only on the grant path**. It exists because a *freshly-minted* SA member isn't yet visible to the IAM policy API (forward 400), or a same-name `deleted:` tombstone hasn't cleared (backward 400), or the governor was *recently* empowered on the resource (403 cache lag). Every one of those is a "the thing I'm pointing at was just created" race.

**Revoke has none of those.** The member being removed is long-established and fully propagated — that's the whole premise (remove the *live* member before deleting the SA, so no tombstone is minted). There's no freshly-created anything. So on the revoke path the propagation loop, both 400 tolerances, and the 403 tolerance **carry no weight** — they're pure imported ceremony.

I applied BCG's "different-API functions stay separate" bullet and used it to justify the duplication, but I skipped the *prior* question BCG says comes first (line 25): **does each element earn its existence at all?** The copied envelope doesn't, in revoke.

The shape revoke actually earns:
- GET policy → `rbgi_jq_remove_member_from_role_capture` (this helper is fine — the one legitimate shared transform) → setIamPolicy.
- Retry only on **409** (etag mismatch = concurrent edit → re-GET) and **transient 5xx/429**.
- Idempotent for free: jq-subtracting an absent member is a no-op, so setIamPolicy just rewrites the same policy → 200.

That's ~40 lines per scope, not 130. Three functions still — project/repo/sa wrap CRM/ArtifactRegistry/IAM, and *that* variation is load-bearing — but each lean. And it means add and revoke are load-bearingly **different** (different failure modes), which is the real reason they stay separate, not "mirror the body one-for-one."

The jq remove helper stays. I'd rewrite the three revoke functions lean — strip the propagation envelope down to GET → remove → setIamPolicy with only the 409/transient retry.

Before I touch it again: is that the non-compliance you're pointing at — the grant-only propagation machinery copied into revoke where it earns nothing? Or are you seeing something else (e.g. that I shouldn't have three separate revoke functions at all)? I'd rather confirm the direction than misread a third time.

156	
157	```bash
158	#!/bin/bash
159	#
160	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
161	#
162	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
163	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
164	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
165	#
166	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
167	#
168	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
169	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
170	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
171	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
172	# limitations under the License.
173	#
174	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
175	#
176	```
177	
178	**Cross-module references need no per-file directive.** Files that reference cross-module `Z*_/RB*_` readonly state trip SC2153 (possible misspelling) and SC2154 (referenced but not assigned). Both are suppressed globally in `busc_shellcheckrc`, applied on the canonical lint path (the project's shellcheck tabtarget always passes `--rcfile`). No inline directive is carried — see **Shellcheck Integration** and **Prohibited Constructs § Inline Shellcheck Directives**.
179	
180	The `set -u` clause of `set -euo pipefail` is the runtime backstop for the same typo class SC2153 catches statically: a misspelled cross-module reference dies at first reference rather than silently expanding to empty.
181	
182	### Tabtarget Path Indirection
183	
184	A tabtarget in `tt/` is a colophon, not a program — it names *what* to run, never *how* to reach it. A single trampoline, `tt/z-launcher.sh`, owns the "how." Every other tabtarget is a declarative stub: a shebang, a `BURD_*` config block, and a byte-identical `exec` into the trampoline.
185	
186	```bash
187	#!/bin/bash
188	export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
189	exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
190	```
191	
192	The exec line carries no launcher token — it is identical, character for character, across every tabtarget. All per-tabtarget variation lives in the `BURD_*` block between the shebang and the exec: which launcher (`BURD_LAUNCHER`, always the first config line), and any dispatch flags (`BURD_NO_LOG`, `BURD_INTERACTIVE`) that follow.
193	
194	The trampoline has exactly two responsibilities:
195	
196	1. **Resolve the moorings location.** The consumer's configuration tree (launchers, regimes) lives under a moorings directory whose path the trampoline holds as a single hardcoded literal. This is the single point of customization at the tabtarget layer — the parallel to BURC at the CLI layer. Move the moorings, edit one line; every tabtarget follows.
197	2. **Normalize cwd to repo root.** The trampoline `cd`s to the repo root before dispatching, so every workbench starts from a deterministic directory regardless of where the user invoked the tabtarget. Downstream code relies on a known cwd rather than reconstructing one.
198	
199	**The launcher name.** `BURD_LAUNCHER` is a bare launcher basename — `launcher.<id>_workbench.sh`. Every launcher co-locates in the moorings launcher directory (`rbml_launchers/`), so the trampoline resolves the basename directly against that one directory and exec's it, forwarding the tabtarget basename and user args unchanged. `BURD_LAUNCHER` is not a private positional convention: it is a regime variable — declared in `burd_regime`, allowlisted in `bud_dispatch` — so the launcher identity travels the same validated `BURD_*` channel as every other dispatch value, and flows downstream unchanged.
200	
201	Why name the launcher in a config variable rather than pass it as a positional dispatch token? **Because the byte-identical exec line is load-bearing.** When the only thing that varies between two tabtargets is their `BURD_*` block, a tabtarget is purely declarative — it states *which launcher and which flags*, and holds no dispatch logic that can drift from the trampoline's expectations. The launcher identity rides the same validated `BURD_*` channel as every other dispatch value rather than a parallel argument slot, and the exec collapses to a true constant the qualifier checks against a single literal.
202	
203	This indirection is the tabtarget-layer instance of CLI as Module Gateway: a tabtarget is external code, and it reaches module functionality only through a launcher the trampoline resolves — never by sourcing or kindling directly. The launcher → dispatch chain it hands off to is what provides the Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables described next.
204	
205	### Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables
206	
207	Three BURD variables carry directory paths across the exec boundary into CLI processes. These are derived from BURC configuration and exported by bud_dispatch.sh during zbud_setup():
208	
209	| Variable | Source | Value |
210	|----------|--------|-------|
211	| `BURD_TOOLS_DIR` | `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` | Project tools root directory |
212	| `BURD_BUK_DIR` | Derived from `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` | BUK subdirectory (`$BURC_TOOLS_DIR/buk`) |
213	| `BURD_TABTARGET_DIR` | `BURC_TABTARGET_DIR` | Tabtarget directory |
214	
215	These variables are available in exec'd CLI processes without requiring the CLI to re-source BURC. CLIs that need stable paths to tools or utilities should use these variables rather than reconstructing paths from `BASH_SOURCE`.
216	
217	### Template 2: CLI Entry Point
218	
219	BUD variables are provided by dispatch. The CLI header sources only `buc_command.sh`; all other dependencies are sourced inside the furnish function. This enables doc-mode help display without runtime env vars — furnish gates after `buc_doc_env` calls before any sourcing that might fail.
220	
221	```bash
222	# «shebang»
223	# «copyright»
224	
225	set -euo pipefail
226	
227	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
228	
229	z«prefix»_furnish() {
230	
231	  # Document only the BUD variables actually used
232	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
233	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
234	  buc_doc_env "«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE " "Module specific configuration file"
235	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
236	
237	  # Source all dependencies
238	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
239	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/«prefix»_«name».sh"
240	
241	  # Source regime files, if any
242	  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source regime file"
243	
244	  z«prefix»_kindle
245	  z«prefix»_enforce  # Regime archetype: ironclad gate after kindle (omit if no regime)
246	}
247	
248	buc_execute «prefix»_ "«module_description_phrase»" z«prefix»_furnish "$@"
249	
250	# eof
251	```
252	
253	### Differential Furnish (conditional dependency sourcing)
254	
255	The furnish function receives the **command name** as `$1`, enabling conditional sourcing based on which command will execute. This allows heavy dependencies (e.g., GCP client libraries, OAuth flows) to be loaded only for commands that need them.
256	
257	**Pattern**: Light dependencies always loaded; heavy dependencies conditionally sourced per command.
258	
259	```bash
260	z«prefix»_furnish() {
261	  local z_command="${1:-}"
262	
263	  # Document environment variables
264	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
265	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
266	  buc_doc_env "«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE " "Module configuration file"
267	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
268	
269	  # Always source light dependencies
270	  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source regime"
271	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source validation"
272	
273	  # Conditionally source heavy dependencies based on command
274	  case "${z_command}" in
275	    «prefix»_survey|«prefix»_audit)
276	      # These commands need GCP and OAuth infrastructure
277	      source "${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/some/heavy_dep.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source heavy deps"
278	      zheavy_kindle
279	      ;;
280	    «prefix»_validate|«prefix»_render)
281	      # These commands work with config only
282	      ;;
283	  esac
284	
285	  z«prefix»_kindle
286	}
287	```
288	
289	**Key points:**
290	- The furnish function receives the command name (e.g., "«prefix»_validate") as `$1`
291	- Existing furnish functions that don't use `$1` are unaffected (extra arg ignored in bash)
292	- Light, always-needed dependencies are sourced unconditionally
293	- Heavy dependencies are sourced only for the commands that need them (avoiding startup overhead)
294	- The `case` statement can be omitted if a module has no conditional dependencies
295	
296	**Benefits:**
297	- Faster startup for lightweight commands (validate, render, list)
298	- Heavy infrastructure (GCP SDKs, OAuth flows) loaded only when actually needed
299	- Reduced memory footprint for simple operations
300	- Clear visibility of which commands depend on what infrastructure
301	
302	### Template 3: Implementation Module
303	
304	```bash
305	# «shebang»
306	# «copyright»
307	
308	set -euo pipefail
309	
310	# Multiple inclusion detection
311	test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
312	Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1
313	
314	# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition — available at source time)
315	# «PREFIX»_some_path="fixed-filename.env"
316	# «PREFIX»_some_prefix="prefix_"
317	
318	######################################################################
319	# Internal Functions (z«prefix»_*)
320	
321	z«prefix»_kindle() {
322	  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» already kindled"
323	
324	  # Validate only the BURD variables actually used
325	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"   # If using temp files
326	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"  # If producing outputs
327	  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}" || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP is unset"  # If using timestamps
328	
329	  # Validate module specific environment
330	  buv_file_exists "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}"  # If present
331	
332	  # Source credentials into environment only - never write to disk
333	  source "${REGIME_CRED_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source credentials"
334	
335	  # Kindle constant (public — safe to read from other modules; never a secret)
336	  «PREFIX»_PUBLIC_SELECTED_NAME="some-specific-name"
337	
338	  # Kindle constants (internal)
339	  Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"  # Non-secret data only
340	  Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_result.json"
341	  Z«PREFIX»_CONFIG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_config_"     # rest of file name appended on use
342	  Z«PREFIX»_MANIFEST_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_manifest_"   # rest of file name appended on use
343	
344	  # Temporary files (if using BURD_OUTPUT_DIR)
345	  Z«PREFIX»_OUTPUT_RESULT="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/result.txt"
346	
347	  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
348	}
349	
350	z«prefix»_sentinel() {
351	  test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module «prefix» not kindled - call z«prefix»_kindle first"
352	}
353	
354	# Helper functions follow
355	
356	######################################################################
357	# External Functions («prefix»_*)
358	
359	«prefix»_«command»() {
360	  z«prefix»_sentinel
361	
362	  local z_param1="${1:-}"
363	  local z_param2="${2:-}"
364	
365	  # Documentation block
366	  buc_doc_brief "«one_line_description»"
367	  buc_doc_param "«required_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
368	  buc_doc_oparm "«optional_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
369	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
370	
371	  # Implementation
372	  test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "Parameter 'param1' is required"
373	
374	  buc_step "«user_visible_milestone»"
375	  buc_log_args "«detailed_progress_information»"
376	  [command] || buc_die "«failure_explanation»"
377	
378	  buc_success "«success_message»"
379	}
380	```
381	
382	**Kindle constant — anti-pattern:**
383	
384	```bash
385	# ❌ Kindle constant defined outside kindle
386	z«prefix»_setup() {
387	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"   # VIOLATION: must be in kindle
388	}
389	
390	# ✅ Kindle constants defined in kindle, used elsewhere
391	z«prefix»_kindle() {
392	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"
393	  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
394	}
395	```
396	
397	**Tinder vs kindle constant — choosing correctly:**
398	
399	```bash
400	# ✅ Tinder constant: pure string, available at source time
401	«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"        # lower_snake name — no kindle needed
402	
403	# ✅ Tinder-on-tinder composition: expansion of earlier tinder constant
404	«PREFIX»_role_director="director"
405	«PREFIX»_fact_ext_director="ext-${«PREFIX»_role_director}"  # resolves at source time
406	
407	# ✅ Kindle constant: depends on runtime state, requires kindle
408	«PREFIX»_CONFIG_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # SCREAMING name — kindle required
409	
410	# ❌ Wrong: runtime variable expansion in tinder position
411	«PREFIX»_config_dir="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # VIOLATION: BURD_TEMP_DIR is runtime — must be kindle
412	
413	# ❌ Wrong: computation in tinder position
414	«PREFIX»_color_red=$(z«prefix»_color '1;31')   # VIOLATION: $() — must be in kindle
415	
416	# ❌ Wrong: expansion of kindle constant in tinder position
417	«PREFIX»_derived="${«PREFIX»_KINDLED_VAR}/sub"  # VIOLATION: KINDLED_VAR available only after kindle
418	```
419	
420	## Variable Handling (General Rules)
421	
422	### Expansion Requirements
423	
424	**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
425	```bash
426	"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
427	"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
428	"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
429	```
430	
431	**Never use:**
432	```bash
433	$var                    # Unquoted - word splitting risk
434	"$var"                  # Unbraced - boundary ambiguity
435	${var}                  # Unquoted - glob expansion risk
436	```
437	
438	**No tilde in quoted strings** — tilde expansion is suppressed inside quotes:
439	```bash
440	# ❌ Tilde does not expand in quotes
441	"~/Downloads/file.txt"          # Literal ~ character, not home directory
442	
443	# ✅ Use $HOME instead
444	"${HOME}/Downloads/file.txt"    # Expands correctly
445	```
446	
447	**Quote inside parameter expansion operators:**
448	```bash
449	# ✅ Inner expansion quoted separately — matches literally
450	"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"       # Strip suffix
451	"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"       # Strip prefix
452	
453	# ❌ Inner expansion unquoted — matches as a glob pattern
454	"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"         # z_suffix with * or ? chars matches as glob!
455	"${z_path#${z_prefix}}"         # Same risk
456	```
457	
458	When using `%`, `%%`, `#`, `##` operators with a variable as the pattern, the inner expansion must be quoted separately. Without inner quotes, the pattern is glob-expanded — a suffix containing `*` or `?` silently matches more than intended.
459	
460	### Assignment Patterns
461	
462	```bash
463	# Direct assignment
464	local z_message="Processing item"
465	
466	# ❌ NEVER declare multiple variables on one line
467	local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!
468	
469	# ✅ ALWAYS one declaration per line with explicit initialization
470	local z_name=""
471	local z_value="default"
472	local z_result=0
473	
474	# ❌ NEVER use local -i — silently coerces non-integer values to 0
475	local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0 — violates no-silent-failures!
476	
477	# ✅ Use plain local with explicit validation
478	local z_count="${z_input}"
479	test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "z_count must be non-negative integer, got: ${z_count}"
480	
481	# ✅ Two-line pattern for captured values (explicit and debuggable)
482	local z_token
483	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
484	
485	# ❌ NEVER combine declaration with capture
486	local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!
487	
488	# From file using builtin $(<file) - NOT command substitution, a bash builtin
489	local z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
490	test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"
491	
492	# Never split declaration from $(<file) assignment
493	# ❌ local z_content
494	# ❌ z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")  # Unnecessary verbosity
495	
496	# Use Capture functions for secrets that must never touch disk
497	local z_token
498	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
499	
500	# Temp files INSTEAD of command substitution holds intermediate results
501	echo "${z_result}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE}"
502	complex_command > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
503	process < "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
504	local z_result=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}")
505	test -n "${z_result}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}"
506	```
507	
508	### Prefer Bash Builtins Over External Tools
509	
510	```bash
511	# ✅ String manipulation with parameter expansion
512	"${z_path##*/}"                    # Instead of: basename
513	"${z_path%/*}"                     # Instead of: dirname
514	"${z_string//old/new}"             # Instead of: sed 's/old/new/g'
515	"${z_string#prefix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/^prefix//'
516	"${z_string%suffix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/suffix$//'
517	
518	# ✅ Reading lines with 'read' — guard handles missing trailing newline
519	while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
520	  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo line"
521	done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"
522	
523	# ✅ Arithmetic with $((...))
524	z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))    # Instead of: expr or bc
525	```
526	
527	### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution
528	
529	All external command output capture uses temp files. The three prohibited `$()` patterns are: `local z_var=$(cmd)` (local swallows exit status), pipelines inside `$()` (hidden intermediate failures), and unguarded `$()` (silent failure). The only `$()` permitted on functions is for `_capture` (secrets that must not touch disk) and `_recite` (read-only roll access) — both with explicit `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`. Bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`) is also permitted in `$()` as no file-based alternative exists.
530	
531	These rules govern **command substitution** — `$(command)`, which spawns a subshell, runs a command, and can fail. They do not apply to `$(<file)`, which despite sharing the `$()` syntax is a bash **builtin file redirect**: no subshell, no command, nothing to fail. `$(<file)` needs no `|| buc_die` guard — it takes content validation (`test -n` on the result) instead, since a successful read of an empty or missing file still yields the empty string.
532	
533	```bash
534	# ❌ Anti-pattern: nested command substitution
535	z_result=$(echo $(complex_command) | process)  # Hidden failures!
536	
537	# ❌ Anti-pattern: unguarded command substitution
538	z_timestamp=$(date +%s)  # Failure is silent!
539	
540	# ✅ Correct: temp files make failures visible afterward
541	complex_command > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
542	process < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
543	read -r z_result < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "No result"
544	
545	# ✅ Correct: single external command via temp file
546	date +%s > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Failed to get timestamp"
547	local -r z_timestamp=$(<"${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}")
548	test -n "${z_timestamp}" || buc_die "Empty timestamp"
549	
550	# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: Secrets use _capture functions, never temp files
551	# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: _recite functions return via stdout (read-only roll access)
552	```
553	
554	**Temp file lifecycle**: Temp files under `BURD_TEMP_DIR` are **preserved after execution** for forensic debugging. Never delete temp files in module code — their persistence is intentional. Cleanup is handled by infrastructure outside BCG's scope.
555	
556	### Stderr Capture — Never Suppress
557	
558	Never use `2>/dev/null` on external commands. Redirect stderr to a temp file so forensic evidence is preserved when commands fail. Include the stderr file path in the `buc_die` message so the user can inspect it.
559	
560	```bash
561	# ❌ Anti-pattern: stderr suppressed — failure cause invisible
562	curl -sS "${z_url}" -o "${z_output_file}" 2>/dev/null \
563	  || buc_die "Failed to fetch ${z_url}"
564	
565	# ✅ Correct: stderr captured to temp file, referenced in die message
566	curl -sS "${z_url}" -o "${z_output_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
567	  || buc_die "Failed to fetch ${z_url} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
568	```
569	
570	**Stderr file naming**: Use a kindle `_PREFIX` constant (grouped with other temp file prefixes for collision visibility) and append a discriminator at the usage site.
571	
572	**In loops**, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness rather than trusting loop data values to be unique:
573	
574	```bash
575	# In kindle — grouped with sibling temp file prefixes
576	Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_"
577	
578	# In command function — integer discriminator ensures uniqueness
579	local z_index=0
580	while IFS='|' read -r z_varname z_image z_tag || test -n "${z_varname}"; do
581	  local z_manifest_file="${Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX}${z_index}_manifest.json"
582	  local z_stderr_file="${Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX}${z_index}_inspect_stderr.txt"
583	
584	  docker manifest inspect "${z_image}:${z_tag}" \
585	    > "${z_manifest_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
586	    || buc_die "Failed to inspect ${z_image}:${z_tag} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
587	
588	  z_index=$((z_index + 1))
589	done
590	```
591	
592	### String Usage
593	
594	```bash
595	# Single-line HERE-string allowed for piping variables
596	jq '.field' <<<"${z_json_data}"
597	grep "pattern" <<<"${z_content}"
598	
599	# Multi-line HERE-strings and HERE-docs prohibited
600	```
601	
602	### Readonly Patterns
603	
604	Readonly enforcement makes mutation bugs loud at the point of violation rather than silent cascades downstream. Three patterns at different scopes:
605	
606	#### `local -r` — Default for Local Variables
607	
608	Use `local -r` for every local variable that is assigned once and never modified. This is the majority of locals in a typical BCG function.
609	
610	```bash
611	# ✅ Default: readonly locals
612	local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
613	local -r z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"
614	local -r z_full_ref="${z_image}@${z_digest}"
615	local -r z_content=$(<"${z_file}")    # $(<file) is a builtin — safe on one line
616	```
617	
618	**Four exceptions** where `local` (mutable) is required:
619	
620	```bash
621	# Exception 1: Loop counters
622	local z_index=0
623	z_index=$((z_index + 1))
624	
625	# Exception 2: Per-iteration synthesized variables (reassigned each pass)
626	local z_manifest_file=""
627	for z_spec in "${z_specs[@]}"; do
628	  z_manifest_file="${Z_PREFIX}${z_index}_manifest.json"
629	  ...
630	done
631	
632	# Exception 3: Two-line capture pattern (readonly would lock empty value)
633	local z_token
634	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
635	
636	# Exception 4: Accumulators and state-tracking flags
637	local z_any_failed=0
638	local z_current_group=""
639	```
640
